title: BES Cyber System Impact Rating Drift Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-002-6 R2.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-002-6 R2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.2: BES Cyber System Impact Rating Drift Detection.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.2' running on index=ot_security asset records, bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
spl:
| inputlookup bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv
| eval key=lower(bes_asset_hostname)
| join type=left key [
    search index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:asset" earliest=-24h
    | eval key=lower(hostname)
    | stats latest(impact_tag) as observed_impact by key
  ]
| where lower(observed_impact)!=lower(impact_rating)
| table bes_system, impact_rating, observed_impact, key
description: We highlight when operational tags on hosts disagree with the registered high or medium impact rating so impact determinations stay defensible.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_security and sourcetypes ot:asset. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Populate `impact_tag` from OT Security Add-on or CMDB sync. (2) Tune mapping for staging assets. (3) Monthly governance report. (4) Drive formal CIP-002 review updates when drift persists. Indexes required: `index=ot_security`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ot:asset`; Lookups: `bes_cyber_system_categorization`, `bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv`.
visualization: Table (mismatches), Single value (drift count), Column chart (by site). Key fields: `bes_system`, `impact_rating`, `key`, `observed_impact`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for we highlight when operational tags on hosts disagree with the registered high or medium impact rating so impact determinations stay defensible. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_security` asset records, `bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate `impact_tag` from OT Security Add-on or CMDB sync. (2) Tune mapping for staging assets. (3) Monthly governance report. (4) Drive formal CIP-002 review updates when drift persists.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv
| eval key=lower(bes_asset_hostname)
| join type=left key [
    search index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:asset" earliest=-24h
    | eval key=lower(hostname)
    | stats latest(impact_tag) as observed_impact by key
  ]
| where lower(observed_impact)!=lower(impact_rating)
| table bes_system, impact_rating, observed_impact, key
```

Understanding this SPL

**BES Cyber System Impact Rating Drift Detection (CIP-002-6 R2)** — We highlight when operational tags on hosts disagree with the registered high or medium impact rating so impact determinations stay defensible.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_security` asset records, `bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **key** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where lower(observed_impact)!=lower(impact_rating)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: bes_system, impact_rating, key, observed_impact.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (mismatches), Single value (drift count), Column chart (by site)
